items.q: What does a correlation of -0.8 indicate?
items.misconception: A weak relationship (because it is negative).
items.why: Students treat the negative sign as 'weak'. The sign shows DIRECTION (as one variable goes up, the other goes down); the absolute value shows STRENGTH. -0.8 is strong.

items.q: Which component handles visual and spatial information?
items.why: Students mix up the slave systems. The phonological loop is for auditory/verbal information; the sketchpad is for visual/spatial. The central executive coordinates but doesn't store.

items.q: What is the capacity of short-term memory according to Miller?
items.why: Students confuse capacity (items) with duration (about 18-30 seconds without rehearsal). Miller's magic number describes how many CHUNKS, not how long.

items.q: Reflecting on how the researcher shapes the data is called:
items.why: Correct answer: Reflexivity. “Triangulation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many phases are in Braun and Clarke's thematic analysis?
items.why: The real figure is 6 phases. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
